label: Contrastive learning

node.label: Contrastive learning
node.definition: Representations are shaped by pulling related examples together and separating unrelated examples.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Contrastive learning is modeled as depending on Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Contrastive and mutual-information objectives.
examples: atlas downstream link to Contrastive and mutual-information objectives
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Contrastive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Self-supervised learning.

In this atlas graph, Contrastive learning has a `uses` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations because Embeddings & distributed representations is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Contrastive learning has a `uses` dependency on Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs because Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Contrastive learning can also be used in supervised or weakly supervised settings when labels define the positive and negative pairs.

sentence_units.text: Representations are shaped by pulling related examples together and separating unrelated examples.

In this atlas graph, Contrastive learning is modeled as depending on Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Contrastive and mutual-information objectives.
